description: This component provides a form for configuring rate limit settings for a specific project. It uses React Hook Form with Zod for validation and Redux for dispatching updates to project settings.

This component serves as the client-side entry point for the schema editor page. It dynamically imports the main content, displaying a detailed skeleton loading state with top bar, canvas, and footer placeholders while the editor initializes.
This module defines the forgot password page. It renders the ForgotPasswordForm component centered on the screen, providing the user interface for initiating a password reset.

This module defines the POST API route for user logout. It clears multiple authentication-related cookies by setting their values to empty strings and their expiration to the past, effectively logging the user out and returning a success message.

This module exports a React component called CodeBlock that renders code snippets using a SyntaxHighlighter component. It provides props for specifying the code content, language, title, and other display options like line numbers.

This module defines the DashboardPage component, which serves as the main dashboard route for authenticated users. It fetches and displays various analytics data using charts and provides a safety check to redirect unverified users.
This module defines an API route for updating user information. It handles POST requests, authenticates users via a cookie, forwards the update request to a backend service, and returns the response.

This route handles GET requests to check the authentication status and synchronize user access status with the backend. It retrieves the auth token from cookies, fetches user data, and sets an 'auth-access-status' cookie based on the user's access status.

This component displays a pending access request message to the user. It periodically checks the access status via an API and redirects the user to the dashboard if approved or to a rejected page if denied.

This component provides the main interface for building API workflows using a visual editor. It integrates React Flow for node-based editing, Redux for state management, and various custom nodes for different API logic.
This component provides a user interface for signing into an account. It handles form validation, dispatches login actions to Redux, and navigates the user upon successful authentication.

This component provides a form for users to initiate the password reset process. It allows users to enter their email address to receive a One-Time Password (OTP) and handles the API call to send the OTP, displaying loading states and success/error messages.

This module defines a reusable React component called BaseHandle, which wraps the Handle component from @xyflow/react. It applies a consistent set of default styles for a handle, allowing for custom class names and children.

This component provides pagination for documentation pages, displaying links to the previous and next articles based on the current path and a predefined navigation structure. It flattens the navigation items to determine the correct order and renders navigation links if available.

This component provides a form for users to verify a One-Time Password (OTP). It handles OTP input, a resend timer, and submission logic for both account verification and password reset flows, integrating with Redux for state management and a backend API for verification.

This page component allows users to select a database (either platform or external) associated with a project to manage its schema. It displays available databases and navigates to the schema management page for the selected database.

This component provides a layout for authentication-related pages, displaying a logo, navigation, and a dynamically selected motivational quote. It ensures a consistent look and feel across sign-in, sign-up, and other auth flows.
This module defines the page for verifying OTP (One-Time Password). It renders a `VerifyOtpForm` component within a Suspense boundary, displaying a loading message while the form is being loaded.

This module defines the sign-in page component, allowing users to log in with email/password or through OAuth providers like Google and GitHub. It handles form submission, client-side validation, and dispatches authentication actions to the Redux store.
This module defines the Reset Password page, serving as a route that renders the ResetPasswordForm component. It provides the user interface for resetting a forgotten password.

This component defines the layout for the request access pages, providing a fixed top bar and a scrollable main content area. It wraps its children with a SidebarProvider, ensuring consistent UI elements across the request access section.

This page component displays a list of databases associated with a selected project, including both internal platform databases and external databases configured via secrets. Users can search, filter, and view databases in either a grid or list layout, with loading states handled by skeletons.

This component displays API documentation for a specific project, allowing users to view and search deployed workflows and their associated documentation. It integrates with Redux for state management and uses various UI components for presentation.

This component provides a form field for selecting the secret type for JWT verification, utilizing react-hook-form and custom UI components for a consistent look and feel. It allows users to choose between 'JWT' and 'Cookie' as the secret type.

This Next.js API route handles the completion of user onboarding. It updates a cookie to reflect the user is no longer new and optionally attempts to notify a backend service about the onboarding completion.

This route handles setting a username for a user after OAuth authentication. It receives an email and username, calls an external API to update the username, and then sets authentication cookies upon success.

This API route handles GET requests to retrieve the current user's details. It authenticates the user using a cookie-based token, fetches data from a backend API, and updates user-related cookies based on the response.
This component provides a form for users to reset their password. It handles OTP input, new password submission, and integrates with an authentication API for password reset functionality.

This component provides a form for configuring Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) settings for a project. It allows users to enable/disable CORS and specify allowed origins, methods, and headers, integrating with Redux for state management and Zod for form validation.

This component provides a search dialog for documentation, allowing users to search through mock data for pages, sections, and code snippets. It uses UI components for dialogs, inputs, and scrollable areas, and displays filtered results based on user queries.

This component provides a form for generating JSON Web Tokens (JWTs). It allows users to input a payload, select a secret type (JWT or Cookie), and specify an expiration time for the token, leveraging react-hook-form for state management and Shadcn UI components for the interface.
raw_content:
"use client";

import { useFormContext } from "react-hook-form";
import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input";
import {
  FormField,
  FormItem,
  FormLabel,
  FormControl,
  FormMessage,
} from "@/components/ui/form";
import {
  Select,
  SelectContent,
  SelectItem,
  SelectTrigger,
  SelectValue,
} from "@/components/ui/select";
import { Textarea } from "@/components/ui/textarea";

export const JwtGenerateForm = () => {
  const { control } = useFormContext();

  return (
    <div className="space-y-3">
      <FormField
        control={control}
        name="payload"
        render={({ field }) => (
          <FormItem className="space-y-1.5">
            <FormLabel className="text-xs font-medium text-neutral-700">
              Payload
            </FormLabel>
            <FormControl>
              <Textarea
                placeholder="Write what payload to be set in the JWT"
                {...field}
                className="text-xs border-neutral-300 min-h-[80px] font-mono"
              />
            </FormControl>
            <FormMessage className="text-[10px]" />
          </FormItem>
        )}
      />
      <FormField
        control={control}
        name="secretType"
        render={({ field }) => (
          <FormItem className="space-y-1.5">
            <FormLabel className="text-xs font-medium text-neutral-700">
              Secret
            </FormLabel>
            <Select onValueChange={field.onChange} defaultValue={field.value}>
              <FormControl>
                <SelectTrigger className="h-8 text-xs border-neutral-300">
                  <SelectValue placeholder="Select a type of secret" />
                </SelectTrigger>
              </FormControl>
              <SelectContent>
                <SelectItem value="jwt" className="text-xs">
                  JWT
                </SelectItem>
                <SelectItem value="cookie" className="text-xs">
                  Cookie
                </SelectItem>
              </S

description: A React component that displays a line chart showing API calls over time. It visualizes time-series data of API call counts within a Card component, providing a clear representation of usage trends or activity.

This route handles POST requests to establish an OAuth session. It receives a token and an optional 'isNewUser' flag, sets multiple authentication-related cookies (auth-token, auth-verified, auth-is-new-user) with a 24-hour expiry, and returns a success response.

This component renders a radial bar chart displaying the success and failed API calls. It visualizes the proportion of successful versus failed calls within a total count, providing a quick overview of API performance.

This component provides a form for configuring parameters, specifically managing a list of 'sources'. It uses react-hook-form for state management and various UI components from a custom library to render input fields, selects, and buttons for adding/removing sources.

This module defines the root layout for the Next.js application, setting up global styles, fonts, metadata, and client-side providers like Vercel Analytics. It wraps the entire application content, providing a consistent structure and theme.

This component renders a bar chart displaying the top projects based on their API call volume. It uses Recharts for charting and Shadcn UI components for the card and chart container, providing a visual representation of project activity on a dashboard.

This route handles POST requests to '/api/access/request'. It authenticates the user using a cookie token, forwards the access request to a backend API, and then updates the user's access status in a cookie if the request is successful.

This component provides the main layout for documentation pages, including navigation, search, table of contents, and theme toggling. It structures the content and provides common UI elements for the documentation section of the application.

This page component is responsible for displaying and managing a specific workflow within a project. It fetches workflow data, handles loading states, and provides an interface for editing the workflow using the ApiBuilderContent component.
A React component that displays a pie chart visualizing the success and failure rates of API calls using Recharts and custom UI components. It shows the total count in the center of the chart.

This component dynamically renders various content types such as headings, paragraphs, and code blocks based on a structured content array. It supports markdown-like inline links within paragraphs and generates unique IDs for headings.

This component provides a form for configuring MongoDB data sources, allowing users to select collections, define query parameters, and manage data fields. It integrates with Redux for state management, fetching collections and their parameters, and utilizes react-hook-form for form handling.

This component renders the account preferences page, allowing users to update their profile information, change their password, and select their preferred theme. It interacts with Redux for state management and an API for user-related operations.
This module defines the API route for user signup. It handles POST requests, forwards the signup data to a backend API, and returns the response or an error to the client.

This page component displays a list of secrets for a specific project. It fetches project and secret data from a Redux store, handles loading states, and provides functionality to edit secrets via a dialog.

This component renders a dynamic navigation menu for documentation, supporting nested items, search filtering, and section expansion/collapse. It highlights the currently active link based on the URL pathname.
This is a loading component for a specific collection within a database secret under a project. It currently renders null, indicating that no specific loading UI is displayed for this route segment.

This module provides the sign-up page for the application, allowing users to create an account using email/password or OAuth providers like Google and GitHub. It handles form validation, user registration, and redirects upon successful signup.
This component provides a user interface for requesting access to a workspace after onboarding. It handles the asynchronous request to an API endpoint and redirects the user based on the outcome.
This route handles POST requests to update a user's theme. It authenticates the user using a cookie token and forwards the theme update request to a backend service.

This page component displays a list of user projects, allowing for creation, searching, and switching between grid and list views. It also integrates a tour guide for new users and manages project data fetching and state.
This component displays a page indicating that a user's access request has been rejected. It provides a message explaining the rejection and a link to contact support.
raw_content:

description: This component renders a custom 404 'Not Found' page for the application. It provides navigation back to the previous page if available in history, or to the dashboard, and displays a large '404' visual along with a friendly message.
This component renders a bar chart displaying the top workflows based on the number of API calls. It uses Recharts for charting and Shadcn UI components for the card and chart container.

This module handles user login requests by forwarding them to a backend API and setting authentication cookies upon successful login. It manages error responses and sets multiple client-readable cookies including an auth token, verification status, and new user status.

This component renders a sidebar for API documentation, displaying a list of deployed workflows. It allows users to select a workflow to view its details and provides visual feedback for the active selection.

This module defines a set of React components for rendering a database schema node, including its header, body, table rows, and cells. It provides a structured way to display database schema information within a larger application.

This component renders the dashboard for a specific project, displaying project details, navigation links, and various charts like success/fail, top workflows, and API calls. It uses Redux to manage project state and ensures the currently viewed project is selected in the global state.
This component renders a form field for selecting an HTTP response status. It integrates with react-hook-form to manage the status selection and provides a list of common HTTP status codes.
raw_content:
"use client";

import { useFormContext } from "react-hook-form";
import {

export const ResponseForm = () => {
  const { control } = useFormContext();

  return (
    <div className="space-y-3">
      <FormField
        control={control}
        name="status"
        render={({ field }) => (
          <FormItem className="space-y-1.5">
            <FormLabel className="text-xs font-medium text-neutral-700">
              Status
            </FormLabel>
            <Select onValueChange={field.onChange} defaultValue={field.value}>
              <FormControl>
                <SelectTrigger className="h-8 text-xs border-neutral-300">

              </FormControl>
              <SelectContent>
                <SelectItem value="200" className="text-xs">
                  200 OK
                </SelectItem>
                <SelectItem value="201" className="text-xs">

                </SelectItem>

                  204 No Content
                </SelectItem>

                  400 Bad Request
                </SelectItem>
                <SelectItem value="401" className="text-xs">
                  401 Unauthorized
                </SelectItem>
                <SelectItem value="403" className="text-xs">
                  403 Forbidden
                </SelectItem>
                <SelectItem value="404" className="text-xs">
                  404 Not Found
                </SelectItem>
                <SelectItem value="500" className="text-xs">
                  500 Server Error
                </SelectItem>

description: This module handles POST requests to change a user's password. It authenticates the user using a cookie token and forwards the request to a backend API, returning the backend's response or an error.

This module defines a set of React components for building a consistent node UI, primarily intended for use within a React Flow application. It includes a base container, header, title, content, and footer components, all styled with Tailwind CSS and designed for reusability.

This component provides the layout for the schema view within a project, handling the fetching and display of database secrets and allowing users to select different database connections. It integrates with Redux for state management and Next.js for routing and parameter handling.

This component provides a standardized page layout for documentation, including a breadcrumb navigation, an optional title and description, and a container for markdown-rendered content within a documentation layout.
This component renders a dynamic breadcrumb navigation based on the current URL pathname. It displays a 'Home' link and then segments of the path, converting them into human-readable titles.

This component renders the application sidebar, providing navigation links for different sections of a project. It integrates with Redux to manage authentication and project selection, dynamically updating navigation based on the currently selected project.
This component provides a client-side syntax highlighter for code snippets, supporting various languages like TypeScript, JavaScript, CSS, JSON, and Bash with customizable styling and line numbering.
raw_content:
"use client"

import { useState } from "react"
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"
import { Check, Copy } from "lucide-react"
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

interface SyntaxHighlighterProps {
  children: string
  language?: string
  title?: string
  className?: string
  showLineNumbers?: boolean
}

// Simple syntax highlighting patterns
const syntaxPatterns = {
  typescript: [
    {
      pattern:
        /\b(const|let|var|function|class|interface|type|enum|import|export|from|default|async|await|return|if|else|for|while|try|catch|finally|throw|new|this|super|extends|implements|public|private|protected|static|readonly)\b/g,
      className: "text-blue-400",
    },
    {
      pattern: /\b(string|number|boolean|object|undefined|null|void|any|unknown|never)\b/g,
      className: "text-green-400",
    },
    { pattern: /"([^"\\]|\\.)*"/g, className: "text-yellow-300" },
    { pattern: /'([^'\\]|\\.)*'/g, className: "text-yellow-300" },
    { pattern: /`([^`\\]|\\.)*`/g, className: "text-yellow-300" },
    { pattern: /\/\/.*$/gm, className: "text-gray-500 italic" },
    { pattern: /\/\*[\s\S]*?\*\//g, className: "text-gray-500 italic" },
    { pattern: /\b\d+\.?\d*\b/g, className: "text-orange-400" },
    { pattern: /[{}[\]()]/g, className: "text-purple-400" },
  ],
  javascript: [
    {
      pattern:
        /\b(const|let|var|function|class|import|export|from|default|async|await|return|if|else|for|while|try|catch|finally|throw|new|this|super|extends)\b/g,
      className: "text-blue-400",
    },
    { pattern: /"([^"\\]|\\.)*"/g, className: "text-yellow-300" },
    { pattern: /'([^'\\]|\\.)*'/g, className: "text-yellow-300" },
    { pattern: /`([^`\\]|\\.)*`/g, className: "text-yellow-300" },
    { pattern: /\/\/.*$/gm, className: "text-gray-500 italic" },
    { pattern: /\/\*[\s\S]*?\*\//g, className: "text-gray-500 italic" },
    { pattern: /\b\d+\.?\d*\b/g, className: "text-orange-400" },
    { pattern: /[{}[\]()]/g, className: "text-purple-400" },

description: This component displays and manages data for a specific collection within a database, allowing users to view, search, edit, and create documents. It handles data fetching from either a platform-specific API or an external database API based on URL parameters.
This component defines the layout for protected dashboard pages, including a fixed topbar, a sidebar, and a main content area. It uses a SidebarProvider to manage the sidebar's open state.

This component acts as a client-side wrapper to conditionally render its children based on the Redux authentication state. It assumes server-side middleware has already handled actual route protection, primarily ensuring client-side UI consistency with the authentication status.
This module defines an API route for updating a user's profile. It acts as a proxy, forwarding profile update requests to a backend service after authenticating the user with an 'auth-token' cookie.

This module defines the main landing page component for the application, featuring a sticky navigation bar, dynamic imports for client-side components, and various sections for features, benefits, and FAQs, utilizing UI components and data from local sources.

This component generates a table of contents from the headings present in the main content area of a page. It dynamically extracts headings, generates IDs if missing, tracks the active section based on scroll position, and provides smooth scrolling navigation.

This component synchronizes the authentication state from browser cookies with the Redux store upon application load. It checks for authentication tokens and user verification status in cookies and dispatches corresponding actions to update the Redux state, including fetching user details if necessary.

This component renders a form for configuring API start parameters, specifically the HTTP method and path. It uses react-hook-form for state management and various UI components from a custom library to display a disabled select input for the method and a disabled text input for the path.

"use client";

import { useFormContext } from "react-hook-form";
import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input";
import {
  FormField,
  FormItem,
  FormLabel,

} from "@/components/ui/form";
import {
  Select,
  SelectContent,
  SelectItem,
  SelectTrigger,
  SelectValue,
} from "@/components/ui/select";

export const ApiStartForm = () => {
  const { control } = useFormContext();

  return (
    <div className="space-y-3">
      <FormField
        control={control}
        name="method"
        render={({ field }) => (
          <FormItem className="space-y-1.5">
            <FormLabel className="text-xs font-medium text-neutral-700">
              Method

              onValueChange={field.onChange}
              defaultValue={field.value}

      <FormField

description: This component renders the application's top navigation bar, displaying user information, project and workflow selectors, breadcrumbs, and handling logout functionality. It integrates with Redux for state management and Next.js hooks for routing.

This component displays a chart of API calls over time, allowing users to select different date ranges (7, 30, or 90 days) to visualize usage patterns. It fetches data from an analytics API and presents it using a Recharts AreaChart.

This component displays API documentation for a given workflow, including endpoint details, descriptions, and code snippets in various languages. It fetches documentation from a Redux store and provides copy functionality for the code snippets.

This module defines the root layout for the Next.js application, setting up global styles, fonts, metadata, and integrating client-side providers, analytics, and a toaster notification system. It acts as the main wrapper for all pages in the application.

This component renders the main content for a schema page, including a ReactFlow canvas for visualizing and interacting with database schemas. It integrates with Redux for state management, handles schema creation, updates, and generation, and provides UI elements for schema manipulation and navigation.

This component renders the settings page for a specific project, allowing users to view and edit project details like name and description, and configure CORS and Rate Limit settings. It integrates with Redux for state management and displays toast notifications for user feedback.

This component provides a user interface for submitting feedback, including issue reports and support requests. It allows users to select a project, issue type, service, severity, and provide a subject and message, integrating with Redux for state management and feedback submission.

This component provides a form for configuring a 'Loop' node, specifically allowing users to define the items to loop through using a suggest field. It integrates with react-hook-form for state management and displays an informational message about dynamic node values.

This module handles the post-OAuth login success flow. It extracts token and user information from URL parameters, dispatches an action to complete the OAuth login, and then redirects the user to either a username setup page (for new users) or the dashboard (for existing users).
This module defines a Next.js page for displaying logs related to a specific project. It retrieves the project ID from the URL parameters and passes it to the LogsPage component.

This module defines the SupportPage component, which serves as a protected route. It renders a FeedbackSheet component within a scrollable container, providing a dedicated support or feedback interface.

This component provides a form field for defining a JavaScript expression as a condition, typically used for dynamic node values. It integrates with react-hook-form and offers a suggestion field for inserting dynamic node values.

This component renders a global sidebar for navigation within the application. It dynamically displays navigation items, fetches user-specific data (dashboard and projects) upon authentication, and collapses/expands based on user interaction, while also hiding itself on project-specific routes.

This component provides a form for configuring database interactions, allowing users to select collections, define query and data parameters, and manage database-related settings within a larger application context. It integrates with Redux for state management and react-hook-form for form handling.

This component provides a form for editing logic code, typically JavaScript or TypeScript, within a larger form context. It includes a text area for code input with dynamic node value suggestions and an informational message.

This module defines the onboarding flow for the application, displaying different screens based on the 'step' query parameter. It includes a splash screen, a series of onboarding questions, and redirects to the dashboard upon completion.

This module exports a React functional component called CodeBlock that renders syntax-highlighted code using the 'prism-react-renderer' library. It takes code, an optional language, and an optional className as props, displaying the code with a dark theme and handling overflow.

This component renders a bar chart displaying the distribution of requests by HTTP method. It uses Recharts for charting and Shadcn UI for card and chart container components, showing a 'No data available' message if the provided data is empty.

This Next.js API route handles OTP verification by forwarding the request to a backend API. Upon successful verification, it sets authentication tokens and user status as non-httpOnly cookies in the response for client-side access.

This component renders a pie chart displaying the distribution of secrets across different providers. It uses Recharts for charting and Shadcn UI for card and chart container components, showing a 'No data available' message if no relevant data is provided.

This page component displays a list of workflows associated with a specific project, allowing users to view, edit, and navigate to individual workflow details. It fetches and manages workflow data using Redux and interacts with various UI components for presentation and user interaction.
This page component displays the execution logs for a specific workflow. It fetches workflow details and log data, allowing users to view individual log details in a side sheet.

This component provides a user interface for managing database collections and documents within a project. It allows users to view, search, edit, and create documents, supporting both project-specific and platform-wide database interactions.
This component renders a bar chart displaying the top workflows based on API calls. It uses Recharts for visualization and includes a fallback message when no data is available.

This component provides a user interface for a user to choose and set their username during an authentication flow. It handles input, validation, and submission of the username to an API endpoint, then redirects the user to an onboarding page.
